title: Understanding LASSO regression
text: Regularization means adding a penalty for model complexity when fitting a model to shrink coefficients and prevent the model from overfitting the training data. LASSO is a type of regularization that penalizes the sum of the absolute values of coefficients, so it can push some coefficients to $$0$$, effectively removing certain features.

title: Understanding Bias
text: High bias in models means the model is too simple and thus can't capture the underlying data. Since LASSO restricts coefficients and can even push some of them to $$0$$, Model $$2$$ has greater complexity than Model $$3$$. In a more complex model (has more features), there is lower bias since the model better captures the original data.
